mapping.a_element: low-energy effective action parameterizing new-particle/photon interactions
mapping.b_element: effective field theory formulation of the dilute Bose gas

mapping.a_element: expansion in the weak coupling of light new degrees of freedom to photons/external fields
mapping.b_element: perturbative expansion in the small gas parameter sqrt(na^3)

mapping.a_element: extraction of underlying microscopic properties from special-purpose setups
mapping.b_element: nonuniversal effects depending on the interatomic potential (e.g. effective range) beyond the scattering length

brief_justification: The only real overlap is the generic effective-field-theory framing (write a low-energy effective action / systematic small-parameter expansion, with universal leading terms and nonuniversal microscopic corrections), which is a meta-methodology shared across most of physics, not a specific transferable mechanism. The actual governing machinery differs entirely: A concerns nonlinear-QED / new-particle photon couplings producing optical signatures in strong electromagnetic fields, whereas B is Bogoliubov-type many-body perturbation theory computing ground-state energy, condensate depletion, and the critical temperature of a weakly interacting condensate, with no shared governing equation, dynamical law, or role-for-role dynamical correspondence.
